codeReview.summary: Worker correctly aligned Screen ID format (SCREEN-<lowerCamelCase>) between SPEC.md, schema.json, and samples. However, the STATE and ROLE ID patterns in SPEC.md's embedded JSON schema were NOT updated, creating inconsistent alignment for those ID types.

issues.description: STATE ID embedded schema pattern is '^STATE-[A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$' (PascalCase only, no hyphens) but schema.json uses '^STATE-[A-Za-z][a-zA-Z0-9-]*$' (allows lowercase and hyphens). Samples use 'STATE-empty-board', 'STATE-validation-error', 'STATE-search-active' which contain hyphens not matched by SPEC's embedded pattern.

ROLE ID embedded schema pattern is '^ROLE-[A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$' (PascalCase only) but schema.json uses '^ROLE-[A-Za-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$' (allows lowercase). Samples use 'ROLE-authenticated' (lowercase 'a' after hyphen) which is not matched by SPEC's embedded pattern.

summary: FAIL: Screen ID format was correctly aligned (SCREEN-<lowerCamelCase>) across SPEC.md, schema.json, and samples. However, STATE and ROLE ID patterns in SPEC.md's embedded JSON schema (lines 614 and 648) were not updated to match schema.json's more permissive patterns. This leaves VAL-SPEC-004 (State ID consistency) and VAL-SPEC-005 (Role ID consistency) unfulfilled. The worker only addressed the SCREEN ID portion of the feature scope as described in the feature description, but the validation contract includes all three ID types.
